content: Just as linguistic reflection (the common meaning of reflexion taught in high school English) requires stepping back to examine how one’s own words reflect intended meaning to an external audience, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing the structure of high school reflective writing exercises: asking LLMs to grade their own outputs against the implicit audience-centered communication norms that reflexion training explicitly teaches. This creates a new structured self-reflection framework grounded in the original pedagogical meaning of the term reflexion, rather than only abstract algorithmic adjustments.
